english: the things related about him) agree, are the same Glr.; ńés-don (nges don), and dráń-don (drang don) v. ńés-pa (nges pa) extr.; idea, notion, conception Was. (283); as the heading of a chapter or paragraph, e.g. sdig-pa dág-pai don (sdig pa dag pa'i don) of the expiation of sin. Rarely in a subjective sense: don-méd byís-pa (don med byis pa) thoughtless children Mil. — 2. the true sense, the real state of the case, the truth, (cf. d. n-dám (don dam)), esp dón-la (don la), sometimes also dón-gyis (don gyis) Tar. 102, 12, in truth, in fact, really Glr. and elsewh.; to speak the truth Thgy.; dón-la bltá-na (don la blta na) col. id.; also for: true! surely! indeed, forsooth. — 3. intent, purpose, design; profit, advantage, _dii don či yin ('di'i don ci yin) what is your meaning and intent (of doing that)? soń-sóń-bai don med (song song ba'i don med) Dzl. going on is to no purpose; don med bźín-du (don med bzhin du) without seeing the use of it, without understanding the purpose Wdn.; with the genit. of the noun: the profit, advantage, the good, of a person, mii don byéd-pa (mi'i don byed pa) to promote a person's welfare; esp. with reference to holy men, _gro(-baí) don byéd-pa ('gro (-ba'i) don byed pa) to work for the welfare of (all) beings, very frq.; of priests col.: to act officially, to sacrifice; gain, profit, v. rnyér-ba (rnyer ba); in a concrete sense: some particular advantage, prerogative, good or blessing obtained, frq.; pán-pai don (phan pa'i don) a useful thing, bdé-bai don (bde ba'i don) a gift of fortune, rnyéd-pa (rnyed pa) to obtain it; dńos-grúb mčóg-gi don (dngos grub mchog gi don) the excellency of the highest perfection; hence dón-du (don du) postp. c. genit. 1. for, for the good or the benefit of; 2. for the sake of, on account of; c. genit. of inf. in order to, that; 3. rarely: in the place of, instead of, against, for, zas nór-gyi dón-du _tsóń-ba (zas nor gyi don du btsong ba) to sell food for money Mil. — 4. in a general sense: affair, concern, business, rań-(gi) don (rang -(gi) don) one's own affairs, one's own interest (cf. n. 3); gźan-(gyi) don (gzhan -(gyi) don) the interest of others; also meton. for disinterestedness Mil. (Ssk. परार्थ); don máń-bas (don mang bas) on account of much business, (syn. brel-bas (brel bas)) Dzl.; chief or main point (ni f.), gsó-ba-rig-pai dón-rnams mdor sdú-ba (gso ba rig pa'i don rnams mdor bsdu ba) to sum up the principal points of medical science; čos don gsúm-la _dús-te (chos don gsum la 'dus te) religion being reduced to three main points (lus (lus), ńag (ngag), yid (yid) Glr.; don sgrúb-pa (don sgrub pa), or _grúb-pa ('grub pa) to settle an affair, to obtain one's end, to attain to happiness. — 5. in anatomy don lńa (don lnga) are: the heart, lungs, liver, spleen, and kidneys Med.; cf. snod (snod). — 6. document, čád-don (chad don) a written contract, agreement; če(d)-don (che(d) don) a letter (to an inferior person). Comp. d. n-ćan (don can), don dań ldán-pa (don dang ldan pa) 1. useful, profitable, expedient, e.g. tsig (tshig) Thgy. 2. enjoying an advantage. 3. having a certain sense. — don-mtún (don mthun) a merchant Cs.; dpal dań ldán-pai don-mtún-dag (dpal dang ldan pa'i don mthun dag) most honourable merchants! — don-dág (don dag) 1. Sch. business, affairs (?). 2. col. = don 1. don-dám (don dam) (परमार्थ), the true sense, subjectively: good earnest, col W. yáńs-pa man don-dám yin (yangs pa man don dam yin) it is not (said in) jest, but in good earnest; objectively: don-dám-par dbyer-méd (don dam par dbyer med) in truth, (after all, upon the whole, in the end), it is all the same Gram.; don-dám rnám-par ńés-pai čos (don dam rnam par nges pa'i chos) Glr. prob. = don-dám-pai bdén-pa (don dam pa'i bden pa) absolute truth Was. (293); in later times = stoń-pa-nyíd (stong pa nyid) Trigl. 20; Mil. — *don-_dás* (*don 'das*) W (lit. _bras ('bras)) = če-dón? (che don?)
num. for bdún-ču (bdun cu), don-yčig (don gcig) 71, 72 etc. to 79.
for _tón-pa ('ton pa) Glr., in one passage, prov. in C.
dob-dób smrá-ba (dob dob smra ba) to talk stuff, nonsense Sch.
the brown bear; dóm-bu (dom bu) 1. Sch. the cub of a bear, 2. Cs.: a species of black dogs, resembling a bear.
Cs.: ornamental fringes hanging down from the neck of a horse; Wdn: mé-tog rtá-yi dom-dóm _dra (me tog rta yi dom dom 'dra).
screen, shade for the eyes and the like Sch.
a pair of draught cattle; glań-dór (glang dor) a yoke of oxen

breeches, trowsers, dor-túń (dor thung) short breeches, dor-ríń (dor ring) long drawers, trowsers Cs.; snam-dor (snam dor) from snam-bu (snam bu); dór-rta (dor rta) 1. that part of the breeches which covers the privy parts, v. rta (rta); gúgs-sa-moi dór-rta des gza sruń, rmá-la pan (gugs sa mo'i dor rta des gza' srung, rma la phan) Wdn., the
